stories.body: Interim president Delcy Rodríguez said 33 people were pulled alive from the rubble over the weekend, including two 11-year-old boys and a father and son found in Caraballeda, but more than 1,450 people are confirmed dead, up to 3,150 are injured and tens of thousands remain missing after the magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 quakes that collapsed roughly 800 buildings across La Guaira state and beyond; desperate families are digging by hand amid complaints of scarce heavy machinery, looting has broken out in La Guaira, mortuaries in Caracas are overwhelmed and aid teams from Colombia, France, the United States and elsewhere are still combing pancaked apartments despite the critical 72-hour window having passed.
stories.headline: Rescuers keep finding survivors days after Venezuela’s twin earthquakes as anger grows over state response

stories.body: After renewed exchanges that included Iranian strikes on US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain and American retaliation against Iranian targets, US officials told CBS News that Washington and Tehran agreed to “stand down” in the Strait of Hormuz and resume talks in Doha, even as Iran studies a broader proposal that would reopen the waterway but insists on maintaining leverage over shipping, access to frozen oil revenues and a ceasefire that also halts Israel-Hezbollah fighting in Lebanon, where Israel continues cross-border strikes despite US mediation and Hezbollah vows to keep attacking until Israeli troops withdraw.
stories.headline: US and Iran reportedly pause strikes around Hormuz while linking broader ceasefire to Lebanon front

stories.body: Iranian foreign minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanon’s Al Mayadeen that any agreement to end the war with the United States and reopen the Strait of Hormuz must include a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah plus a pullout of Israeli forces from Lebanese territory, while advisers to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei warned Israel against resuming strikes on Beirut as Hezbollah, other Iranian-backed militias and even Houthi fighters continue proxy operations that Western analysts say Tehran views as vital to its regional strategy despite heavy losses in recent clashes.
stories.headline: Tehran ties Hezbollah support to any US deal, demanding Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon

stories.body: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said over 1,300 excess deaths since 21 June are tied to Europe’s early summer heatwave, with France alone estimating about 1,000 additional fatalities in four days as Germany, Poland, Czechia and Hungary smashed national temperature records of 40–41.9°C, power grids strained, festivals were cancelled, Berlin police deployed water cannon to cool crowds, and authorities urged residents to stay indoors while warning that climate change is turning “once-in-a-generation” heat into an annual threat.
stories.headline: Europe’s record-breaking heatwave linked to more than 1,300 excess deaths as 40°C temperatures spread east

stories.body: Pakistan’s information minister Attaullah Tarar said air and ground operations in Afghanistan’s Paktia, Paktika and Kunar provinces killed 25–29 militants linked to recent cross-border attacks, including one on a paramilitary post in Karachi, but the Taliban government condemned the raids as “cowardly,” claiming up to 100 people were killed or wounded, many of them civilians in Mandikhel village, underscoring the collapse of last year’s ceasefire and the ongoing dispute over Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan fighters sheltering on Afghan soil.
stories.headline: Pakistan says it killed dozens of militants in Afghan strikes as Taliban accuse Islamabad of civilian deaths

stories.body: Five astronauts briefly sheltered in their SpaceX Crew Dragon capsule when an air leak on Russia’s Zvezda service module doubled to roughly two pounds of air per day and Roscosmos prepared to saw into a suspected crack, a method NASA mission control opposed before reversing its evacuation order once the Russian team paused repairs; Roscosmos says one of two leaks has been sealed and there is no immediate threat, but the incident highlights tensions over station maintenance as US lawmakers debate legislation to extend ISS operations to 2032.
stories.headline: NASA rescinds safe-haven order on ISS after dispute with Roscosmos over sealing growing air leak

stories.body: Kyiv officials urged residents into shelters as missiles and debris ignited fires in the Podil district, torched cars in Obolon and damaged a nine-story apartment block near a kindergarten, a day after President Volodymyr Zelenskyy warned of a possible mass Russian strike and as Moscow vowed to target command centers in retaliation for a Luhansk dormitory attack it blamed on Ukraine, which denies responsibility.
stories.headline: Kyiv weathers intense Russian barrage amid threats of “systematic” strikes on Ukrainian capital

stories.body: A burnover on the merged Snyder Mesa complex killed three members of federal interagency crews and seriously injured two others as extreme heat and high winds pushed more than 28,000 acres of fire across the Colorado-Utah border, prompting Colorado Governor Jared Polis to activate the National Guard and Utah Governor Spencer Cox to ban fireworks while the Cottonwood Fire near Thompson Springs ballooned past 93,000 acres with zero containment.
stories.headline: Three federal firefighters die in Colorado-Utah wildfires as Snyder Fire becomes nation’s largest blaze

stories.body: In a 52-47 vote, Senate Republicans advanced a Department of Homeland Security supplemental that pours tens of billions into Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Border Patrol deportation efforts over the next three years but rejected bipartisan attempts to permanently kill a controversial fund that Democrats call a slush fund for Donald Trump’s allies, relying instead on acting Attorney General nominee Todd Blanche’s pledge not to use it even as moderate Republicans warn the issue could haunt them in November.
stories.headline: Senate backs $70 billion boost for ICE while leaving Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund untouched

stories.body: A new Mississippi law taking effect Wednesday empowers the state’s top law-enforcement agency to compile a list of all immigrants lacking legal status, an open-ended mandate that immigrant advocates in Jackson say could dovetail with President Trump’s mass-deportation plans and chill cooperation with police, even as supporters claim it will improve public safety.
stories.headline: Mississippi to launch statewide registry of undocumented immigrants, raising fears of deportation dragnet

stories.body: The government’s April-to-June program to bring irregular workers out of the shadows closed with roughly 900,000 applications—360,000 already admitted for provisional work and residency permits—far above the half-million the Sánchez administration anticipated, leaving officials three months to resolve each case as they promise fresh data this week on approvals and rejections.
stories.headline: Spain’s extraordinary regularization drive receives 900,000 applications, far exceeding government forecasts

stories.body: With internal dissent muted, the prime minister told the PSOE’s Federal Committee he alone will decide when to call national elections and has no intention of advancing them to 2026, using the meeting to pivot into what aides describe as a long campaign through 2027 even as he taunted PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo for now wanting to “turn the page” on the Catalan procés.
stories.headline: Pedro Sánchez turns PSOE federal council into early launchpad for 2027 campaign despite corruption pressure

stories.body: Junts per Catalunya joined PP and Vox last week to back a motion demanding Pedro Sánchez step down and to sink a Transport Ministry decree with Catalan investments, marking the fifth government decree blocked since January as spokesperson Miriam Nogueras warned Sánchez must leave or “gift the government to the far right,” underscoring how the party is stress-testing the leverage of its seven deputies in a chamber where government defeats now affect nearly a quarter of votes.
stories.headline: Junts withholds votes to force Sánchez’s resignation, deepening paralysis in Congress

stories.body: Following May’s election that left the Andalusian PP with 53 of 109 seats—two short of an outright majority—regional leader Juan Manuel Moreno enters his third investiture debate still negotiating solely with Vox for the votes he needs, as the fragmented parliament’s three leftist groups hold 41 seats and Vox’s alignment remains the decisive factor.
stories.headline: Juanma Moreno faces third investiture debate without Vox deal after losing absolute majority in Andalusia

stories.body: Mossos d’Esquadra say a motorist expelled from a nearby hotel garage sped the wrong way down Carrer de Pallars under the influence of drugs, slammed into another car at the junction with Carrer Fluvià just before 10:00 a.m. Sunday, killed both drivers, severely injured a passenger, wrecked six parked motorcycles and required Barcelona’s fire brigade to extricate all three occupants.
stories.headline: Driver fleeing police kills two and injures one in head-on crash on Carrer de Pallars

stories.body: Interior minister Núria Parlon and sports minister Berni Álvarez detailed a multilevel plan involving Mossos d’Esquadra units, 900 municipal officers, helicopters, drones and a CECOR command post in Egara to manage more than 350 kilometers of road closures across 64 municipalities during the 4–6 July Tour de France stages, with 1,500 officers focused on mobility as the promotional caravan alone will require restrictions lasting over four hours.
stories.headline: Catalunya to deploy 4,500 police and security staff for Tour de France Grand Départ

stories.body: Residents, historians and the Catalan Water Agency report a noticeable fall in the Rec Comtal’s flow near Vallbona and Montcada i Reixac, a change they say did not occur even during drought, and attribute it to impermeable screens erected by the public utility ATL while doubling a main pipeline at the Besòs River’s Pont de la Roca; authorities expect the disruption to be temporary and will monitor whether the usual 500 liters per second return once the works finish in about a month, mindful of the canal’s role in future metropolitan water supplies and local ecological restoration projects.
stories.headline: Historic Rec Comtal canal sees drop in flow blamed on Besòs pipeline works
